title: Crispy Zesty Egyptian Fish Bite-Sized Delights
description: A citrusy and crispy Egyptian dish featuring bite-sized fish pieces coated with a light cornstarch mixture and spherified for a fun and unique texture.
instructions: Cut the cod fillets into small bite-sized pieces and season with salt and black pepper.
In a shallow dish, mix together the cornstarch, lemon juice, orange juice, garlic powder, and a pinch of salt.
Dip each piece of cod into the cornstarch mixture, coating it evenly.
Heat the ghee or vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
Carefully place the coated cod pieces into the hot oil and fry until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the fried cod from the oil and place it on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.

To spherify the vinegar and water mixture, combine it in a small saucepan with 1g of sodium alginate and heat it over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and forms a gel-like consistency.
Using a spoon or piping bag, drop small amounts of the spherified mixture into a bath of cold water to form small balls.
Once the balls have formed, remove them from the water and place them on a plate or tray.
To assemble the dish, place a few pieces of fried cod on a serving plate and top with a few spherified balls.
